narrative.headline: 2018 Honda Odyssey: electrical complaints lead, but the safety systems carry the serious outcomes
narrative.summary: Electrical system failures are the most commonly reported issue, with owners describing complete loss of power — engine, instruments, and accessories cutting out simultaneously at highway speeds. Engine behavior around the idle-stop feature and erratic forward collision avoidance system responses together account for a large share of additional complaints. Powertrain shutdowns round out the pattern, with some incidents occurring while the vehicle was in motion on the highway.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle shutting down entirely while driving at highway speed, with the engine, DVD player, and instrument panel all going dark at once. · 1 fire · 4 injured

issues.sample: OUR 2018 HONDA ODYSSEY ELITE HAS A FEATURE CALLED IDLE STOP.  THE FEATURE HAS MANY CRITERIA THAT MUST BE MET BEFORE IT WILL ACTIVATE.  IT SHOULD JUST TURN OFF THE ENGINE WHEN THE CRITERIA HAVE BEEN MET IN AN EFFORT TO CONSERVE FUEL IN LIEU OF IDLING.  WE ARE SEEING A RANDOM ISSUE
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report unexpected behavior tied to the idle-stop feature, which is designed to cut the engine when certain driving conditions are met but appears to activate or behave in ways that concern drivers. · 1 fire · 2 injured

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report the adaptive cruise control system abruptly decelerating the vehicle without warning, including when attempting to pass other vehicles after pulling out of the lane behind them. · 4 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report complete vehicle shutdowns at highway speed, during which the engine and all onboard systems lose power simultaneously. · 3 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Lane Departure
issues.blurb: 117 reports (13%), tied to 1 crash and no injuries. The least reported of the top five areas.
